chapter_title: Spec. Provisions & Exceptions for Oversize or Overweight Vehicles
text:
In this subchapter:

(1) “Automobile transporter” has the meaning assigned by 49 U.S.C. Section 31111.

(2) “Backhaul” means the return trip of a vehicle transporting cargo or general freight.

(3) “Stinger-steered” means a truck-tractor and semitrailer combination in which the fifth wheel is located on a drop frame located behind and below the rearmost axle of the truck-tractor.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 622. Spec. Provisions & Exceptions for Oversize or Overweight Vehicles
##### § 622.151 — Definitions

In this subchapter:

    (1) “Automobile transporter” has the meaning assigned by 49 U.S.C. Section 31111.

    (2) “Backhaul” means the return trip of a vehicle transporting cargo or general freight.

    (3) “Stinger-steered” means a truck-tractor and semitrailer combination in which the fifth wheel is located on a drop frame located behind and below the rearmost axle of the truck-tractor.

*Added by Acts 2021, 87th Leg., R.S., Ch. 411 (S.B.1815), Sec. 1, eff. September 1, 2021.*

subsections.text: “Automobile transporter” has the meaning assigned by 49 U.S.C. Section 31111.
“Backhaul” means the return trip of a vehicle transporting cargo or general freight.
“Stinger-steered” means a truck-tractor and semitrailer combination in which the fifth wheel is located on a drop frame located behind and below the rearmost axle of the truck-tractor.
